highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.133 Released: New Worktree Base Branch Control
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.133 adds a `worktree.baseRef` setting that lets you choose whether new worktrees branch from `origin/<default>` (fresh) or your local `HEAD`. This gives teams more control over how isolated agent workspaces are created, especially when using `--worktree` or `EnterWorktree`. Note that the new default `fresh` changes existing `EnterWorktree` behavior, so check your workflows if you rely on it.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.132: Session ID in Bash Tools and Alternate Screen Opt-Out
highlights.summary: This release exposes `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` inside Bash tool subprocesses so your scripts and hooks can identify which session they belong to. It also adds `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ALTERNATE_SCREEN=1` for developers who prefer the conversation to stay inline rather than switching to a fullscreen renderer. Both are environment variable toggles requiring no code changes.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest iteration of its most capable model tier. Developers using Claude Code can expect improved reasoning and code generation quality when this model is available in the CLI. Check the Anthropic blog for capability details and pricing before updating your model configuration.

highlights.title: Uber Spent Its Entire 2026 AI Budget on Claude Code in Four Months
highlights.summary: Uber reportedly burned through its full-year 2026 AI tooling budget on Claude Code usage within just four months, highlighting how quickly token costs can escalate when teams adopt AI coding assistants at scale. This is a cautionary tale for engineering managers and a reminder to set usage budgets and monitor spend proactively. If you're rolling out Claude Code across a team, consider rate limits and cost guardrails from day one.

highlights.title: Amnitex: Lossless Memory Layer for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Amnitex is an open-source project that adds a persistent, lossless memory layer on top of AI coding assistants like Claude Code, so context isn't lost between sessions. This is especially useful for long-running projects where you need Claude to remember decisions, file structures, and conventions without re-explaining them each time. Worth exploring if you find yourself repeatedly providing the same background context.

highlights.title: Complete 60-Minute Claude Code Masterclass (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This comprehensive Chinese-language Bilibili tutorial covers Claude Code from initial installation all the way through advanced usage patterns, and includes a full reference document. The creator spent three weeks producing the guide, making it one of the most thorough resources available for Chinese-speaking developers. Beginners and intermediate users alike will find actionable walkthroughs for everyday coding tasks.

highlights.title: How to Build an App With Claude Code – Full Tutorial for Beginners
highlights.summary: This YouTube walkthrough takes you through the entire process of building a real application using Claude Code, from project setup to a working product. It's designed for beginners who want a practical, project-based introduction rather than a feature overview. Following along with your own project idea is a great way to internalize the workflow.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: A focused 30-minute YouTube tutorial aimed at developers who want to get productive with Claude Code quickly without sitting through a multi-hour course. It covers the most impactful features and workflows so you can start using Claude Code effectively in your daily development right away. A great option if you already know basic programming but are new to AI-assisted coding.

tips_of_the_day: Set a monthly token spend alert in your Anthropic console before rolling Claude Code out to your whole team — uncapped usage can exhaust budgets surprisingly fast, as large engineering orgs are discovering.

Use the new `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ALTERNATE_SCREEN=1` environment variable if you work in terminals like Emacs `eat` or tmux panes where the fullscreen renderer interrupts your layout — the conversation will stay inline and scrollable.

Store repeated project context (architecture decisions, naming conventions, file structure) in a `CLAUDE.md` file at your repo root so Claude Code picks it up automatically every session, saving you from re-explaining the same background each time.
